requirement.title: Scan Existing Specs Before Starting
requirement.description: Brainstorming MUST scan the specs/ directory for existing spec_ids before beginning any elicitation, to detect potential iteration scenarios.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a specs/ directory containing specs/auth/spec.xml and specs/payments/spec.xml, brainstorming MUST present both spec_ids (auth, payments) to the user before proceeding. REQ-F001
Given an empty specs/ directory, brainstorming MUST proceed directly to new-spec flow without presenting any existing spec_ids. REQ-F001

requirement.title: Confirm Spec Target (New or Iteration)
requirement.description: After scanning, brainstorming MUST ask the user to confirm whether this is a new spec or an iteration on an existing spec. The confirmation determines which conditional sections appear in the design doc output — it does not invoke a separate code path or "mode". Brainstorming has one behavior with context-sensitive output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a similar spec found, brainstorming MUST ask: "Iterating on <spec-id> (v<N> active)?" and wait for user confirmation before proceeding. REQ-F002
Given no matching spec, brainstorming MUST propose a spec-id and ask: "New topic — proposed spec-id: <suggestion>. OK?" before proceeding. REQ-F002
Brainstorming MUST NOT assume the target without user confirmation. The confirmation is explicit, not inferred. REQ-F002

requirement.title: Output Format When No Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: When no prior spec.xml exists for the target spec-id, brainstorming produces a complete design doc at the canonical location. The refiner consumes the entire design doc — no structured delta section is needed because there is nothing prior to compare against.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Output MUST be written to docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md. REQ-F003
The design doc MUST contain substantive design content: problem description, proposed solution, identifiable requirements (in prose), and scope (what's included and excluded). REQ-F003

requirement.title: Read Prior Spec and Iteration History Before Eliciting a Change
requirement.description: When iterating on an existing spec, brainstorming MUST read the prior spec.xml and all previous design iterations before eliciting the change from the user. This ensures the change intent is informed by accurate current state, not by user recollection.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given specs/my-feature/spec.xml at v2, brainstorming MUST read the spec.xml content during Phase 1 (Understanding) before asking the user about changes. REQ-F004
Given two previous iterations (2026-01-01, 2026-03-15), brainstorming MUST read both design docs to understand the evolution history. REQ-F004

requirement.title: Output Format When Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: When iterating on an existing spec, brainstorming produces a design doc whose primary content is a Context summary + natural-language Change Intent. The refiner reads this design doc alongside the existing spec.xml and derives the structured <delta> itself — no explicit delta section is authored in the design doc.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The design doc MUST include a Context section summarizing the current spec scope in 2-3 sentences, with a reference to the spec.xml version. REQ-F005
The design doc MUST include a Change Intent section describing what is changing and why. This is the primary input for the refiner to derive the delta. REQ-F005
The design doc SHOULD include an Architecture Impact section describing how changes interact with existing design. Optional for simple changes. REQ-F005

The design doc MUST NOT include a pre-authored structured delta section (no explicit ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED lists). The LLM-based refiner performs the diff; the design doc carries only human-authored narrative intent. REQ-F005

requirement.title: Spec ID Derivation and Confirmation
requirement.description: When no prior spec exists, the spec_id MUST be derived from content at the end of Phase 2 (when scope is clear) and confirmed by the user before output. For iterations, the spec_id is inherited from the prior spec — no derivation step is needed.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The spec_id MUST NOT be finalized before Phase 2 completes. REQ-F006
The user MUST explicitly confirm or modify the proposed spec_id before the design doc is written. REQ-F006

requirement.title: Output Validation — Design Doc Structure
requirement.description: Before writing the design doc to disk, brainstorming MUST validate that it conforms to the design doc contract (fr-cc-if-001).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: When no prior spec exists, output MUST be validated as follows: file path follows docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md convention, design doc has substantive content (not empty/stub), identifiable requirements and scope present in prose. ERROR on any missing element. REQ-F003

When a prior spec exists, output MUST be validated as follows: Context section present (with spec version reference), Change Intent section present, no pre-authored structured delta section. ERROR on missing required sections. REQ-F005

requirement.title: Phase 0 — Pending-Conflict Detection and Iterate-Branch Auto-Entry
requirement.description: Brainstorming's Phase 0 scan-and-route logic MUST check for the presence of specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md (the ephemeral handoff written by refiner per fr-rf-015 on R3 axis-1/2/3 block). If the file exists, brainstorming MUST automatically enter its iterate branch with the conflict body as Change Intent seed — the user does not need to retell the conflict. The user picks a candidate resolution (or describes their own); brainstorming writes a new dated docs/plans/<spec-id>/<NEW-DATE>/design.md with the resolution baked in; the pending file MUST be deleted on successful design write so zero authoritative state remains across the conflict cycle (R3 holds — refiner never wrote design.md; brainstorming did, after user authorization; the ephemeral marker existed transiently and is now removed).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md exists at the start of brainstorming Phase 0, brainstorming MUST automatically enter the iterate branch (without asking the user "new spec or iteration?") and load the file's conflict_description, candidate_resolutions, and cited design line ranges / Q&A q_ids as Change Intent seed. 2026-04-27:B.3

Brainstorming MUST present the candidate resolutions to the user verbatim from the pending file and prompt for selection ("pick (a), (b), (c), or describe your own"). The user does not retell the conflict; brainstorming does not re-derive the conflict from scratch. 2026-04-27:B.3

Once the user selects (or describes) a resolution and brainstorming successfully writes the new design.md to docs/plans/<spec-id>/<NEW-DATE>/design.md, brainstorming MUST delete specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md. If the new-design write fails, the pending file MUST NOT be deleted — cleanup is gated on successful write. 2026-04-27:B.3

Brainstorming's Phase 0 conflict-detection branch MUST NOT modify or rewrite the _pending-conflict.md content — the file is read-only from brainstorming's perspective. Any changes to the conflict framing happen in the new design.md, not by editing the handoff file. 2026-04-27:B.3

requirement.title: Structured Decision-Log Output Format
requirement.description: Brainstorming Phase 2 (eliciting Q&A) MUST emit the Q&A history as a structured decision-log conforming to fr-cc-if-008. The decision-log is consumed by refiner for Phase 4 axis 2 contradiction check (design ↔ Q&A) and Phase 6 mechanical authorization check (axis 3 trace verification). The previous free-form decision-log.md format (where rows had no stable identifiers and refiner could not mechanically iterate over Q&A rows by q_id) is replaced by the structured format.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each Q&A row in the decision-log MUST carry the four required fields per fr-cc-if-008: q_id (stable identifier, unique per brainstorming session), question (verbatim text of the question asked), user_answer_verbatim (verbatim text of the user's answer), and deferral_signal (boolean — true when the answer matches deferral phrases like "use defaults", "covered.", "skip", "you decide", or similar; false otherwise). 2026-04-27:B.1

The decision-log MUST be machine-parseable per fr-cc-if-008. The wire format (YAML, strict markdown table, etc.) is an implementation choice that brainstorming's writer and refiner's parser MUST agree on; the contract this requirement captures is the field shape and machine-parseability, not the format. 2026-04-27:B.1

q_id values MUST be stable across the brainstorming session — if a question is asked once, its q_id MUST NOT change in later revisions of the same decision-log within the same session. This stability is what enables refiner's traces (per fr-rf-010-ac5) to cite Q&A rows by q_id and have those traces remain valid. 2026-04-27:B.1

Brainstorming MUST detect deferral signals canonically: at minimum the phrases "use defaults", "covered.", "skip", "you decide" set deferral_signal=true. Implementations MAY extend the canonical list with additional deferral phrases, provided the extensions are documented alongside the format implementation. 2026-04-27:B.1
